content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string SSS. A substring of SSS is obtained by removing zero or more characters from the beginning and/or end of SSS. For each LLL (1‚Č§L‚Č§‚ą£S‚ą£1 \le L \le |S|1‚Č§L‚Č§‚ą£S‚ą£):


KKK times, Chef wants to choose a substring of SSS with length LLL uniformly randomly and independently.
Find the probability that all these KKK substrings will be pairwise distinct. It can be proved that this probability can be expressed as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äč, where PPP and QQQ are integers and QQQ is coprime with 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should compute P‚čÖQ‚ąí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where Q‚ąí1Q^{-1}Q‚ąí1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of ‚ą£S‚ą£|S|‚ą£S‚ą£ over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a string SSS followed by a space and an integer KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing ‚ą£S‚ą£|S|‚ą£S‚ą£ integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be P‚čÖQ‚ąí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚čÖQ‚ąí1 for L=iL = iL=i.
